# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.
# Category: kong_no_auth

_format_version: "2.1"

services:
  - name: users-service
    host: users-upstream # Refers to the upstream defined below for load balancing
    protocol: http
    port: 80 # Default port for the service, actual target ports are defined in targets
    path: /api/v1/users # Base path to prepend to requests for this service
    retries: 5
    connect_timeout: 60000
    write_timeout: 60000
    read_timeout: 60000
    tags:
      - microservice
      - users-management

  - name: products-service
    url: http://products-backend.internal:8081 # Direct URL for a simpler service without an explicit upstream
    retries: 5
    connect_timeout: 60000
    write_timeout: 60000
    read_timeout: 60000
    tags:
      - microservice
      - products-catalog

routes:
  - name: users-route
    service: users-service
    paths:
      - /users # Incoming path at the API Gateway
    strip_path: true # Remove /users from the request path before sending to service
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - DELETE
    hosts:
      - api.example.com
      - dev.api.example.com
    tags:
      - public-api
      - users

  - name: products-route
    service: products-service
    paths:
      - /products
    strip_path: true
    methods:
      - GET
    hosts:
      - api.example.com
    tags:
      - public-api
      - products

upstreams:
  - name: users-upstream
    algorithm: round-robin # Load balancing algorithm
    slots: 100 # Maximum number of targets in the upstream
    healthchecks:
      active: # Active health checks
        http_path: /health # Endpoint to check
        timeout: 1 # Timeout for health check request
        unhealthy: # Conditions for marking a target as unhealthy
          http_statuses: [500, 503]
          tcp_failures: 2
          timeouts: 3
          interval: 5 # Interval between unhealthy checks
        healthy: # Conditions for marking a target as healthy
          http_statuses: [200]
          successes: 3
          interval: 5 # Interval between healthy checks
    tags:
      - load-balancer
      - users

targets:
  - upstream: users-upstream
    target: users-backend-1.internal:8080 # IP or hostname and port of an instance
    weight: 100 # Higher weight means more requests
    tags:
      - production
      - users-instance

  - upstream: users-upstream
    target: users-backend-2.internal:8080
    weight: 100
    tags:
      - production
      - users-instance

  - upstream: users-upstream
    target: users-backend-3.internal:8080
    weight: 50 # Example: a less powerful instance gets half the traffic
    tags:
      - production
      - users-instance
